params.name: Talmud
params.description:
On Nov 11, 308 AD, the Talmud was first compiled into a book.
The Talmud is an important text of Rabbinic Judaism. Written in Mishnaic Hebrew, it records various contents such as Jewish laws, customs, and ceremonies. It also contains a large number of topics on history, folklore, astronomy, geography, medicine, etc. It was foundational to all Jewish thought and aspirations and became the primary source of Jewish religious law that had been passed down from generation to generation.
